relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Temple authorities arrive as witness is still being spoken.
They are troubled by resurrection announced in Jesus.
The witnesses are held overnight.
Yet the heard word multiplies trust.
Rulers, elders, and scribes gather in Jerusalem.
The high-priestly household surrounds the inquiry.
The central question is power and name.
Peter speaks filled with holy Spirit.
The examined deed is the saving/healing of a weak human.
The name of Jesus Messiah stands the healed one whole.
The rejected stone has become the corner-head.
No other name carries this necessary saving/healing.
Bold ordinary people are recognized as having been with Jesus.
The healed person standing makes denial impossible.
The council confers privately.
The sign is known and cannot be denied.
Threat tries to stop the name from spreading.
They command silence in Jesus’ name.
Peter and John appeal to hearing the Divine over human order.
Witness cannot not speak what it has seen and heard.
Threats end in release because the people glorify the Divine.
The healed one’s long years make the sign undeniable.
Released witnesses return to their own and report.
The community lifts one voice to the Creator.
Psalm and Spirit interpret raging nations.
Rulers gather against the Lord and Messiah.
Herod, Pilate, nations, and Israel are named in the city’s resistance.
Human plots are held within divine purpose.
They ask not for escape but bold speech.
The hand of healing and the name of Jesus continue signs.
Prayer shakes the place and fills them again with Spirit-boldness.
The trusting multitude becomes one heart and one life.
Resurrection witness carries great power and great grace.
Need is answered by shared possessions.
Distribution follows need.
Barnabas appears as Son of Encouragement.
He sells a field and lays the silver at apostolic feet.
